title: pelargonium × clementinae
intro: Pelargonium × clementinae, also known as Clementine Rose Geranium, is a hybrid plant species within the Pelargonium genus, characterized by its distinctive rose-like fragrance and attractive foliage. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its essential oil composition. The health benefits of Pelargonium × clementinae include reducing anxiety and stress, promoting relaxation, and soothing digestive issues, making it a popular ingredient in natural remedies. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and essential oil blends, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
